text: When the user says "ascii galaxy" or "/galaxy" they mean the pi TUI extension ~/.pi/agent/extensions/void-blackhole.ts, not the ~/Developer/galaxy website (which is the lensed-blackhole personal site).
Subagents spawned via the subagent tool run in read-only mode by default, so tasks requiring file writes or process spawning must either be run by the lead directly or the write mode enabled first.

When a spawned subagent reports "Read-only mode: run /write" it means child sessions inherited the parent pi session's read-only state — don't re-delegate hoping it clears; verify with a local write test and apply the subagent's returned plan directly.
When spawning pi programmatically (RPC/print/json mode) for tests, pass --write, because the read-only-default extension disables edit/write tools in every new session.
In pi read-only/confirm mode, avoid any `>` in bash commands (including `2>/dev/null`) because the write-gate extension's redirect regex flags all redirections as destructive and blocks the command.

When a subagent reports file state that conflicts with the lead's earlier read, re-verify the actual file content directly before ruling — files can change mid-session and the lead's memory of a file goes stale.

Before delegating via the subagent tool, check the actual active lead model, not settings.json's defaultModel — if the live session is running as claude-sonnet-5, subagent calls are hard-blocked ("Sonnet is already the mechanical/builder tier") and the lead must do the work directly per the Delegation Gate.

When saving a harness-level lesson (about pi itself, delegation, tooling), pass scope "global" explicitly — the default project scope files it under the cwd's git root (falling back to ~), where it won't inject into other projects.
